steps.title: Substitute to simplify the repeating expression
steps.content:
Notice that $(3x - 8)$ appears twice in the equation. Substitute $u = 3x - 8$ to transform it into a standard quadratic:
$$u^2 - 11u + 28 = 0$$

steps.title: Factor the quadratic
steps.content:
Find two numbers that multiply to 28 and add to $-11$: these are $-7$ and $-4$. So:
$$(u - 7)(u - 4) = 0$$
This gives $u = 7$ or $u = 4$.

steps.title: Substitute back and solve for x
steps.content:
Replace $u = 3x - 8$ in each solution:
  • When $u = 7$: $3x - 8 = 7 \Rightarrow 3x = 15 \Rightarrow x = 5$
  • When $u = 4$: $3x - 8 = 4 \Rightarrow 3x = 12 \Rightarrow x = 4$
Both $x = 4$ and $x = 5$ are solutions to the original equation.
